component.name: Dialog
component.description: Centered modal overlay for short, focused, non-destructive interactions that do not require navigating away from the current page.

useCases: Editing a small set of fields for a record (name, email, status)
Confirming a non-destructive action (export, duplicate, publish)
Displaying a read-only summary or detail view (payment receipt, transaction detail)
Presenting a focused prompt that needs a response before the user continues (rename, assign)

commonPatterns.name: edit-form
commonPatterns.description: Short edit form with header, input fields, cancel and save buttons
commonPatterns.composition:
<Dialog>
  <DialogTrigger asChild><Button variant="outline">Edit details</Button></DialogTrigger>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Edit merchant details</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>Changes take effect immediately.</DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
    <Field><FieldLabel>Name</FieldLabel><FieldContent><Input /></FieldContent></Field>
    <DialogFooter>
      <DialogCancel>Cancel</DialogCancel>
      <DialogAction>Save changes</DialogAction>
    </DialogFooter>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

commonPatterns.name: single-action
commonPatterns.description: Informational dialog with a single dismiss action — no cancel button needed
commonPatterns.composition:
Payment processed The payment was successfully captured.
...summary content...
Done

commonPatterns.name: no-footer
commonPatterns.description: Header and body only — no action buttons. User dismisses via X or clicking outside.
commonPatterns.composition:
Keyboard shortcuts Use these shortcuts to navigate faster.
...shortcut list...

commonPatterns.name: compact-confirmation
commonPatterns.description: Small-size dialog with no header for a quick non-destructive prompt
commonPatterns.composition:
Export all transactions as a CSV file?

Cancel Export

antiPatterns.scenario: Using Dialog for destructive or irreversible actions
antiPatterns.reason: Dialog is dismissible by clicking outside; destructive actions must use AlertDialog, which forces explicit acknowledgment and cannot be accidentally dismissed
antiPatterns.alternative: Use AlertDialog for delete, remove, disconnect, or any action that cannot be undone

antiPatterns.scenario: Using Dialog for multi-step or lengthy workflows
antiPatterns.reason: Dialogs are designed for short, focused tasks. Multi-step flows, wizards, or workflows with many fields create cramped, confusing modals and poor mobile experience
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a dedicated full-page route or flow for multi-step processes

antiPatterns.scenario: Rendering only a DialogCancel with no DialogAction
antiPatterns.reason: A dialog with only a cancel button serves no purpose; the X and overlay click already dismiss the dialog
antiPatterns.alternative: If there is only one action, use DialogAction alone. If there are no actions, omit DialogFooter entirely

antiPatterns.scenario: Nesting a Sheet or another Dialog inside DialogContent
antiPatterns.reason: Stacking overlays creates confusing focus trapping and z-index issues
antiPatterns.alternative: Redesign the flow as a full-page route or replace with a Sheet

antiPatterns.scenario: Using Dialog for navigation or routing
antiPatterns.reason: Dialogs do not update the URL and their state is lost on refresh; they are not appropriate for content that should be linkable or bookmarkable

slots.trigger: DialogTrigger wrapping the element that opens the dialog
slots.title: DialogTitle for the accessible dialog heading (required for screen readers)
slots.description: DialogDescription for supporting muted body text
slots.header: DialogHeader — optional top section wrapping title and description. Includes right padding so title never overlaps the X button.
slots.footer: DialogFooter — optional edge-to-edge bottom strip. Place DialogCancel (outline) before DialogAction (primary).
slots.action: DialogAction — primary button; closes dialog on click. Use for the confirming/submitting action.
slots.cancel: DialogCancel — outline button; closes dialog on click. Only render alongside DialogAction.
slots.close: DialogClose — low-level Radix primitive for custom dismiss triggers outside the footer.

parentConstraints: DialogAction and DialogCancel must be inside DialogFooter
DialogTitle must be inside DialogContent (required for accessibility)

interactions.open: Overlay fades in with backdrop blur; content zooms in with fade
interactions.close: Content zooms out with fade; overlay fades out
interactions.dismiss: Clicking the overlay or pressing Escape closes the dialog without any action
interactions.x-button: Always present in DialogContent; closes the dialog on click
interactions.action: DialogAction closes the dialog after performing its operation
interactions.cancel: DialogCancel closes the dialog without performing any operation

responsive.mobile: Centered modal; DialogFooter stacks buttons vertically (column-reverse order)
responsive.desktop: Centered modal; sm max-width 20rem, default max-width 32rem; footer buttons inline, right-aligned

accessibility.keyboardSupport: Escape closes the dialog; Tab cycles focus within dialog; Shift+Tab cycles backwards; Enter/Space activates focused button
accessibility.screenReader: Announced as a dialog with title and description; focus trapped within content; X close button has aria-label 'Close'
accessibility.focusManagement: Focus is trapped inside the dialog when open; returns to the trigger element on close

rules: Use Dialog only for short, single-step interactions. If a task requires multiple steps or significant input, use a full-page route instead.
Never use Dialog for destructive or irreversible actions — use AlertDialog, which cannot be accidentally dismissed by clicking outside.
DialogHeader and DialogFooter are both optional. A dialog can have header only, footer only, or neither.
When there is only one action, render DialogAction alone — do not include DialogCancel.
DialogCancel must only appear alongside DialogAction. Never render it alone.
DialogAction defaults to variant='primary' and size='sm'; DialogCancel defaults to variant='outline' and size='sm'.
DialogTitle is always required inside DialogContent for screen reader accessibility, even if visually hidden.
Uses ModalBase wrapper for the frosted glass container effect; do not replicate this manually.

aiHints.context: Use Dialog for short, focused, non-destructive interactions that overlay the current page: editing a few fields, viewing a summary, or confirming a safe action. It is dismissible via X button or clicking outside. Do NOT use for destructive actions (AlertDialog), multi-step workflows (full-page route), or supplementary panels (Sheet). If in doubt about scope: if it takes more than one screen of content or more than one conceptual step, it belongs in a full-page flow, not a Dialog.
